name: chown
description: Change the user and/or group ownership of a given file, directory, or symbolic link

generators.custom:
function(r,t){return l(this,null,function*(){let i=r.find(e=>e.includes(":")),s=r.find(e=>/^-.*n.*/.test(e)),o;return i?o=yield t("dscl . -list /Groups PrimaryGroupID | tr -s ' '| sort -r"):o=yield t("dscl . -list /Users UniqueID | tr -s ' '| sort -r"),o.split(`
`).map(e=>e.split(" ")).map(e=>({name:s?e[1]:e[0],description:i?`Group - ${s?e[0]:`gid: ${e[1]}`}`:`User - ${s?e[0]:`uid: ${e[1]}`}`,icon:i?"\u{1F465}":"\u{1F464}",priority:90}))})}

options.name: -f
options.description: Don't report any failure to change file owner or group, nor modify the exit status to reflect such failures

options.name: -h
options.description: If the file is a symbolic link, change the user ID and/or the group ID of the link itself

options.name: -n
options.description: Interpret user ID and group ID as numeric, avoiding name lookups

options.name: -v
options.description: Cause chown to be verbose, showing files as the owner is modified

options.name: -R
options.description: Change the user ID and/or the group ID for the file hierarchies rooted in the files instead of just the files themselves

options.name: -H
options.description: If the -R option is specified, symbolic links on the command line are followed

options.name: -L
options.description: If the -R option is specified, all symbolic links are followed

options.name: -P
options.description: If the -R option is specified, no symbolic links are followed
